task_completion.description: Verify the agent upscaled the simple model rocket design by a factor of 2.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenRocket GUI showing the rocket design. The dimensions should reflect a 2.0x upscale (e.g., if the original nose cone was 0.1m, it should now be 0.2m). There may be a 'Scale' dialog visible showing a factor of 2.0.

task_completion.description: Verify the motor mount was manually corrected to a 38mm inner diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the component configuration window for the motor mount (inner tube). The 'Inner diameter' field should be explicitly set to 38 mm (or equivalent in cm/inches).

task_completion.description: Verify a flight simulation was run using a 38mm H-class or I-class motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Flight simulations' or 'Motors & Configuration' tab in OpenRocket. There should be a configuration using an H or I motor (e.g., H165, I200) and a completed simulation run showing an apogee altitude.

task_completion.description: Verify the modified rocket design is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenRocket window title bar showing 'upscaled_2x.ork' or a 'Save As' dialog saving the file to '~/Documents/rockets/upscaled_2x.ork'.

task_completion.description: Verify the upscale report was written with all required documentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor showing 'upscale_report.txt' saved in '~/Documents/exports/'. The text must include: 1) original and new overall lengths, 2) original and new body tube diameters, 3) the specific correction made to the motor mount (38mm), and 4) the chosen motor's simulated apogee altitude.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenRocket GUI to perform the scaling and simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenRocket application open and being interacted with, rather than the agent just writing an XML .ork file manually in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the simulation results appear genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket flight simulation tab should show realistic calculated values (velocity, altitude, etc.) corresponding to an H or I class motor, indicating the software actually ran the math.

integrity.description: Verify the data in the text report matches the GUI results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee and dimensions typed into 'upscale_report.txt' should match the numbers visible in the OpenRocket GUI screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output paths are '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/upscaled_2x.ork' and '/home/ga/Documents/exports/upscale_report.txt'. The source file is 'simple_model_rocket.ork'. Note: The programmatic verifier for this task contains known bugs (it incorrectly expects a nose cone length of 0.24-0.27m instead of the mathematically correct 0.2m, and fails to find the motor mount radius due to XML tag differences). Ignore these programmatic bugs; visually verify that the agent correctly applied a 2.0x scale (resulting in a 0.2m nose cone) and set the motor mount inner diameter to 38mm as requested in the prompt.
